content:
# Replace CIELab with OKLab in CUDA Grade Pipeline

## Decision

Replaced CIELab D65 with OKLab (Björn Ottosson, 2020) in the per-pixel grading pipeline for better perceptual uniformity in color manipulation (ambiance, warmth, vibrance stages).

## Key Details

- OKLab output rescaled to CIELab-compatible numeric ranges (L×100, a×300, b×300) so all tuned manipulation constants remain unchanged
- CUDA sRGB gamma switched from `powf` to `__powf` fast intrinsic (~2× faster on SFU, ~2 ULP accuracy, invisible at 10-bit)
- Combined estimated improvement: 15-30% faster on LAB stage, 5-10% overall per-frame

## Files Changed

- `crates/dorea-color/src/lab.rs` — Rust CPU OKLab conversion
- `crates/dorea-gpu/src/cuda/kernels/grade_pixel.cuh` — CUDA OKLab + `__powf`
- `crates/dorea-gpu/src/cuda/mod.rs` — CPU/GPU parity tolerance adjustments (2→5/255 max)

## Implementation Notes

- The standard OKLab LMS→RGB inverse matrix (row 3: `0.0209, -0.7034, 1.6825`) is NOT the proper inverse of the forward RGB→LMS matrix. Corrected to: `-0.0042, -0.7034, 1.7076` via numpy.linalg.inv. Both Rust and CUDA use the corrected values.
- Function signatures (`srgb_to_lab`, `lab_to_srgb`) unchanged — downstream callers unaffected.
- `cpu.rs` and `postprocess.cu` required no changes (they call the swapped functions).
- CPU/GPU parity tolerances loosened from 2/255 to 5/255 due to `__powf` vs `powf` divergence.

## Branch

`feat/oklab-grade-pipeline` — 3 commits (c04783f, 670bad3, b61a40f)

## Benchmark (POC, not production)

Standalone 4K benchmark of the color transfer step only (Python PyTorch vs Triton):
- CIELab fp32: 33.08ms (30.2 fps)
- OKLab + torch.compile + fp16: 6.81ms (4.9×)
- OKLab fused Triton kernel: 2.89ms (11.4×)

These numbers reflect kernel launch overhead elimination, not directly comparable to production CUDA which is already fused.

## Spec

`docs/decisions/2026-04-09-oklab-grade-pipeline.md`
